mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity multiples, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, depressing equity prices.
failure_condition: Joint index of INDPRO and HOUST shows no predictive power for SPX direction over 20-day horizons, with directional accuracy below 52% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising inflation expectations fail to be offset by higher rates, causing real rates to spike and equity valuations to contract sharply.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Real rates (CPIAUCSL minus FEDFUNDS) show no negative correlation with SPX 5-day returns during identified tightening episodes, or correlation coefficient exceeds -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, mechanically lifting equity index levels over the following quarter as cash flow projections are revised upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns, or rolling correlation remains below 0.10 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105, supply chain bottlenecks ease and capacity utilization normalizes, reducing input cost inflation and supporting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO crosses above 105 compared to periods below that threshold, with t-statistic below 1.65.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with stable unemployment indicates a robust labor market that sustains consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the near term.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE stability fails to predict SPX direction better than a random coin flip over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: In a credit contraction regime, falling residential investment leads household wealth destruction and reduced consumption, which transmits to lower corporate revenues and negative equity returns.
regime_hint: housing market downturn with credit tightening
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns with statistical significance, or the predictive R-squared remains below 0.02 in rolling regressions.
